description: A benchmark evaluating LLM agents on neuroimaging tasks. Platform-agnostic: tasks specify a scientific goal, a public dataset, and a ground-truth reference; agent outputs are graded against the reference.

schema.structure: Tasks nested within category divisions; flat within each category.

categories.data-handling: Converting and organizing raw data into standard formats (DICOM->BIDS, DICOM->NIfTI).
categories.structural: Anatomical T1w/T2w analysis: reconstruction, segmentation, morphometry, registration.
categories.functional: fMRI processing: preprocessing, denoising, connectivity, activity metrics.
categories.diffusion: Diffusion MRI: preprocessing, tensor/FOD modeling, tractography, connectomes.
categories.clinical: Clinical/pathology workflows: lesion and subfield segmentation, clinical preprocessing.
categories.electrophysiology: EEG/MEG processing (property-graded; no external ground truth).

agent_grader_split.prompt: AGENT-VISIBLE. goal (scientific WHAT only), dataset (identity), required_output (artifact + format so it is gradable, not how). required_tool only if the task is intrinsically named.
agent_grader_split.solution: GRADER-ONLY, never shown to agent. ground_truth (+location), metric, pass_criterion, tool_mode, tool_suggestions (NON-BINDING - agents may use novel/future tools), notes.

schema.tool_philosophy: Tools are SUGGESTIONS, never gate grading, EXCEPT intrinsically-named tasks (tool_mode=named) where the tool is part of the goal. Grading is always output-vs-ground-truth, so novel/future tools that produce correct output pass.
schema.anti_gaming: Enforced by the SCORER (not per-task): (1) artifact must exist with correct shape/format [catches no-op/fabrication], (2) artifact must be structurally valid [mask binary, matrix symmetric], (3) artifact must match hidden ground truth by metric [catches wrong-but-plausible]. Ground truth is hidden from agents. required_output per task anchors the existence/shape check.

clinical_motion_t1.notes: Markerless prospective motion-correction study (Cimbi). 22 HEALTHY ADULTS (19-36y). Multi-contrast: 3D T1 MPRAGE (22), FLAIR (10), T1 STIR (22), T2 TSE (22), T2* GRE (19), DWI (10, under derivatives). Each sequence acquired as still + nod (+ shake for T1) deliberate-motion variants. NOT pediatric, NOT longitudinal. MOTION-TASK POTENTIAL: the still/nod/shake structure makes this a strong candidate for motion-robustness / motion-QC tasks not yet in the benchmark

spine_single.notes: Spinal Cord MRI, single-subject style, 19 subj

_osf_groundtruth_main.title: the platform LLM-Agent Benchmark - Ground Truth Data
_osf_groundtruth_main.note: Main GT project for fMRI + remaining-group references. Organized by category with slug-named task folders (e.g. structural-brain-extraction, functional-postprocessing-denoising). Companion to y2dq4 (MRtrix DWI) and q7v8c (QSIPrep/QSIRecon). Data on OSF, code/definitions on GitHub the benchmark repository.

data-handling.description: Converting and organizing raw data into standard formats (DICOM->BIDS, DICOM->NIfTI).

data-handling-dicom-to-bids.title: DICOM to BIDS organization (full subject)
data-handling-dicom-to-bids.description: Organize the DICOM data of subject 001 from the ReproIn/HeuDiConv example dataset into BIDS format using an automated converter (e.g. HeuDiConv).

prompt.goal: Organize the DICOM data for subject 001 of the ReproIn/HeuDiConv example dataset into a valid BIDS dataset, including the anatomical (T1w), the resting-state functional (BOLD), and the field-map series.
prompt.required_output: A valid BIDS dataset (NIfTI images with JSON sidecars, organized per the BIDS standard).

solution.ground_truth: No reference file: validate output BIDS with bids-validator (spec compliance).
solution.pass_criterion: Output is a valid BIDS layout (passes basic BIDS structure checks); dataset_description.json present at the root; Anatomical T1w (NIfTI + JSON) organized for subject 001; Resting-state functional BOLD (NIfTI + JSON) organized for subject 001; Field-map series organized under fmap/; A session sub-directory (ses-1) may be present depending on the conversion tool; No conversion errors; BIDS structure validation passes (dataset_description.json present; sub-XX/ layout correct); NIfTI + JSON sidecar pairs present for each converted series; No processing errors; logs captured
solution.dataset_fetch_reference: datalad install https://datasets.datalad.org/repronim/heudiconv-reproin-example && cd heudiconv-reproin-example/REPROIN/dicom/data-handling-dicom-to-bids && datalad get -J 4 "Patterson_Coben - 1/anatT1w_acqMPRAGE_6" "Patterson_Coben - 1/fmap_acq4mm_7" "Patterson_Coben - 1/fmap_acq4mm_8" "Patterson_Coben - 1/func_taskrest_16"
solution.expected_files_detail: A valid BIDS dataset directory containing dataset_description.json at the root; Per-subject subdirectories (sub-XX/) with modality folders and NIfTI+JSON pairs; participants.tsv and applicable *_events.tsv files

data-handling-dicom-to-nifti.title: DICOM to NIfTI Conversion (dcm2nii)
data-handling-dicom-to-nifti.description: Convert the DICOM files for subject 001 (downloaded via the fetch command) to NIfTI (.nii.gz) with BIDS-style JSON sidecars

prompt.goal: Convert the T1-weighted (MPRAGE) anatomical scan for subject 001 of the ReproIn/HeuDiConv example dataset to NIfTI (.nii.gz) with a BIDS-style JSON sidecar.
prompt.required_output: A NIfTI image (.nii.gz) with a JSON sidecar containing the acquisition metadata.

solution.ground_truth: No reference file: verify NIfTI header/dims/affine against source DICOM.
solution.pass_criterion: A valid.nii.gz is produced from the subject-001 T1w MPRAGE series; A BIDS-style JSON sidecar is produced alongside it; NIfTI geometry is consistent with the source DICOM (3D MPRAGE volume); No processing errors; command output/logs captured; NIfTI produced with correct geometry; JSON sidecar present with acquisition metadata; No processing errors; logs captured
solution.dataset_fetch_reference: datalad install ///repronim/heudiconv-reproin-example && cd heudiconv-reproin-example && datalad get "REPROIN/dicom/data-handling-dicom-to-bids/Patterson_Coben - 1/anatT1w_acqMPRAGE_6"
solution.expected_files_detail: One or more NIfTI files (.nii.gz, ); JSON sidecar(s) with acquisition metadata

structural.description: Anatomical T1w/T2w analysis: reconstruction, segmentation, morphometry, registration.

structural-brain-age-prediction.title: Brain age prediction from T1w
structural-brain-age-prediction.description: Predict brain age (numerical, years) from the T1w of sub-0011 of OpenNeuro dataset ds002790 using a pretrained model; compare to chronological age.

prompt.goal: Predict brain age from the T1-weighted image of sub-0011 using a pretrained brain-age model. Report the predicted brain age as a numerical value in years.
prompt.required_output: The predicted brain age (and/or brain-age gap) as a numeric value in years, in a tabular text format (CSV/TSV) one row per subject.

solution.ground_truth: participants.tsv "age" column, row sub-0011 (chronological age). Compare predicted brain age numerically; brain-age gap = predicted - chronological. Prediction must come from imaging, not be read from participants.tsv.
solution.pass_criterion: T1w preprocessed as the brain-age model requires; Pretrained brain-age model applied; Predicted brain age reported as a numerical value (years); Value in a plausible adult range; No processing errors; logs captured
solution.output_validation: Numeric: predicted brain age vs chronological age (report gap). Plausible adult range.
solution.tool_note: Context: healthy. Currently brainageR is the only brain-age model available; kept agnostic so additional models (pyment, DeepBrainNet) can be added later. python models installable via pip if needed
solution.dataset_fetch_reference: datalad install https://github.com/OpenNeuroDatasets/ds002790.git && cd ds002790 && datalad get sub-0011/anat/

structural-brain-extraction.title: Brain extraction (skull stripping)
structural-brain-extraction.description: Skull-strip the T1w of sub-0004 of OpenNeuro dataset ds002790, producing a brain-extracted image and a binary brain mask in native T1w space.

prompt.goal: Skull-strip the T1-weighted image of sub-0004, producing a brain-extracted image and a binary brain mask in the native T1w space.
prompt.required_output: A skull-stripped brain image (NIfTI) and a binary brain mask (NIfTI) in native T1w space.

solution.ground_truth: derivatives/fmriprep/sub-0004/anat/sub-0004_desc-brain_mask.nii.gz (NATIVE T1w space - no MNI resampling needed since the task is native-space skull-stripping). Dice.
solution.pass_criterion: A skull-stripped brain image and a binary brain mask are produced; Mask geometry (dimensions, voxel size, affine) matches the input image; Brain mask is binary (values 0 and 1 only); Brain mask is anatomically plausible: skull/dura removed, brain retained; No processing errors; logs captured
solution.output_validation: Dice between agent brain mask (or implied mask from skull-stripped output) and the native fmriprep brain_mask.
solution.tool_note: Context: healthy. Tool names from the reference tool comparison.md. All tools verified registry
solution.notes: [note] Native-space mask IS available in fmriprep derivatives, so no MNI-space requirement and no need to generate GT.
solution.dataset_fetch_reference: datalad install https://github.com/OpenNeuroDatasets/ds002790.git && cd ds002790 && datalad get sub-0004/anat/
solution.expected_files_detail: A skull-stripped brain image (NIfTI, any filename — tool-determined; e.g. *_bet.nii.gz, *BrainExtractionBrain.nii.gz, or caller-named via -o); A binary brain mask (NIfTI, any filename — tool-determined; e.g. *_bet_mask.nii.gz, *BrainExtractionMask.nii.gz, *_mask.nii.gz, or caller-named via -m)

structural-cortical-morphometry.title: Cortical ROI morphometry (thickness/area/volume)
structural-cortical-morphometry.description: Compute per-ROI cortical morphometry (thickness, surface area, volume) from an existing FreeSurfer recon for sub-0008 of OpenNeuro dataset ds002790.

prompt.goal: From the pre-computed FreeSurfer reconstruction of sub-0008, compute per-region cortical morphometry - mean thickness, surface area, and grey-matter volume - for each cortical parcel in both hemispheres.
prompt.required_output: A table of per-region cortical morphometry (thickness, surface area, volume), in a tabular text format (CSV/TSV/text).

solution.ground_truth: derivatives/fs_stats/data-cortical_type-aparc_measure-{thickness,area,volume}_hemi-{lh,rh}.tsv (row for sub-0008). BOTH hemispheres (lh + rh), aparc (Desikan-Killiany) atlas. Numeric per-ROI comparison.
solution.pass_criterion: Desikan-Killiany (aparc.stats) per-ROI thickness, area, and volume extracted from the FreeSurfer stats files; Features aggregated into a structured table (CSV/TSV) with headers and numeric values; Row/column counts consistent with the DK atlas region set (~34 cortical ROIs per hemisphere); No processing errors; logs captured
solution.output_validation: Per-ROI numeric comparison of thickness/area/volume, lh and rh, vs fs_stats.
solution.tool_note: Context: healthy. FreeSurfer stats parsing (e.g. aparcstats2table) or direct parse of aparc.stats; nibabel/pandas in Python for table assembly
solution.notes: [input_isolation] Agent parses mri/surf/label; must NOT read the existing stats/ files (GT).
solution.dataset_fetch_reference: datalad install https://github.com/OpenNeuroDatasets/ds002790.git && cd ds002790 && datalad get sub-0008/anat/ && datalad get derivatives/freesurfer/sub-0008/
solution.expected_files_detail: Cortical morphometry statistics tables (any filename; e.g. *aparc_stats.txt or CSV)

structural-cortical-reconstruction.title: FreeSurfer cortical reconstruction (recon-all)
structural-cortical-reconstruction.description: Full FreeSurfer recon-all cortical surface reconstruction and subcortical segmentation from the T1w of sub-0001 of OpenNeuro dataset ds002790.

prompt.goal: Run a full FreeSurfer cortical reconstruction (recon-all) on the T1-weighted image of sub-0001, producing cortical surfaces, the subcortical segmentation, and the cortical parcellation.
prompt.required_output: A cortical surface reconstruction (white and pial surfaces), a subcortical segmentation, and a cortical parcellation.

solution.ground_truth: derivatives/freesurfer/sub-0001/mri/aseg.mgz (subcortical) + aparc+aseg.mgz (cortical parcellation). Compare agent recon to these via Dice on labels + volume correlation. FreeSurfer.mgz -> read with nibabel/mri_convert.
solution.pass_criterion: Cortical reconstruction completed; subject directory with surf/, mri/, stats/ present; White and pial surfaces produced for both hemispheres; Cortical parcellation (aparc+aseg) and subcortical segmentation (aseg.mgz) produced; aseg.stats and per-hemisphere aparc.stats tables produced; No processing errors; recon-all/FastSurfer log captured
solution.output_validation: Dice on aseg labels, volume correlation on aparc regions vs the bundled reference recon.
solution.tool_note: Context: healthy. FreeSurfer full recon-all; FastSurfer alternative is its own task (structural-fastsurfer-recon)
solution.dataset_fetch_reference: datalad install https://github.com/OpenNeuroDatasets/ds002790.git && cd ds002790 && datalad get sub-0001/anat/
solution.expected_files_detail: A FreeSurfer/FastSurfer subject directory (any name) with surf/, mri/, stats/ subdirectories; White and pial surfaces: surf/lh.white, surf/rh.white, surf/lh.pial, surf/rh.pial; Cortical parcellation: mri/aparc+aseg.mgz (or aparc.DKTatlas+aseg.mgz); Subcortical segmentation: mri/aseg.mgz; Statistics: stats/aseg.stats and stats/lh.aparc.stats, stats/rh.aparc.stats; Cortical thickness maps: surf/lh.thickness, surf/rh.thickness

structural-fastsurfer-recon.title: FastSurfer rapid recon-all alternative
structural-fastsurfer-recon.description: Run FastSurfer deep-learning cortical reconstruction on the T1w of sub-0009 of OpenNeuro dataset ds002790, producing the whole-brain segmentation.

prompt.goal: Run FastSurfer (deep-learning cortical reconstruction) on the T1-weighted image of sub-0009, producing the whole-brain segmentation (DKT-atlas cortical parcellation + subcortical structures).
prompt.required_output: A cortical surface reconstruction (white and pial surfaces), a subcortical segmentation, and a cortical parcellation.

solution.ground_truth: derivatives/freesurfer/sub-0009/mri/aparc.DKTatlas+aseg.mgz (under mri/, not stats/). FastSurfer uses the DKT atlas so compare to the DKTatlas reference. Per-label Dice.
solution.pass_criterion: FastSurfer full reconstruction completed (segmentation + surface stream); aparc.DKTatlas+aseg segmentation produced (note: FastSurfer uses the DKT atlas, not DK); White and pial surfaces with thickness produced for both hemispheres; Stats tables produced; No processing errors; logs captured
solution.output_validation: Per-label Dice vs FreeSurfer aparc.DKTatlas+aseg (FastSurfer approximates FreeSurfer; expect high but not perfect overlap).
solution.tool_note: Context: healthy. FastSurfer full recon (run_fastsurfer.sh, both --seg and --surf). Default atlas is DKT (aparc.DKTatlas+aseg), NOT the recon-all DK aparc+aseg filename
solution.dataset_fetch_reference: datalad install https://github.com/OpenNeuroDatasets/ds002790.git && cd ds002790 && datalad get sub-0009/anat/
solution.expected_files_detail: A FastSurfer subject directory with mri/, surf/, stats/ subdirectories; Cortical parcellation + subcortical segmentation: mri/aparc.DKTatlas+aseg.mgz; White and pial surfaces for both hemispheres (surf/lh|rh.white, surf/lh|rh.pial); Cortical thickness (surf/lh|rh.thickness) and stats (stats/aseg.stats, stats/lh|rh.aparc.DKTatlas.stats)

structural-mni-registration.title: MNI152 registration (linear + nonlinear)
structural-mni-registration.description: Register the T1w of sub-0006 of OpenNeuro dataset ds002790 to MNI152 space (linear + nonlinear), producing the registered image and the transform.

prompt.goal: Register the T1-weighted image of sub-0006 to MNI152 standard space using linear then nonlinear registration. Produce the MNI-space registered image and the estimated transform.
prompt.required_output: Registered/resampled NIfTI in target space; Transformation file (any format:.mat, warp.nii.gz,.h5 — tool-dependent)

solution.ground_truth: IMAGE: derivatives/fmriprep/sub-0006/anat/sub-0006_space-MNI152NLin2009cAsym_desc-preproc_T1w.nii.gz (compare via spatial correlation / overlap). TRANSFORM: sub-0006_from-T1w_to-MNI152NLin2009cAsym_mode-image_xfm.h5 (compare the warped result rather than raw h5 fields - apply both transforms to a grid and compare displacement).
solution.pass_criterion: Linear (affine) registration to MNI152 performed; Nonlinear registration performed (warp field produced); T1w resampled into MNI152 space (output dims match the MNI template); Affine matrix and nonlinear warp/transform files produced; No processing errors; logs captured
solution.output_validation: Primary: registered image vs reference MNI T1w (correlation/overlap). Secondary: transform equivalence via applied-displacement comparison.
solution.tool_note: Context: healthy. Target = MNI152. Both linear and nonlinear are required (this is what the task tests). The specific MNI template version and tool are the agent's choice; skills guidance can inform that selection
solution.dataset_fetch_reference: datalad install https://github.com/OpenNeuroDatasets/ds002790.git && cd ds002790 && datalad get sub-0006/anat/

solution.pass_criterion: Thickness/area/volume statistics exported for standard parcellations; Tables contain numeric values for expected structures; No processing errors; logs captured
solution.output_validation: Numeric comparison of per-region thickness/area/volume vs the reference stats tables.
solution.tool_note: Context: healthy.
solution.notes: [input_isolation] CRITICAL: the agent must parse mri/surf/label to compute stats, NOT read the existing stats/ files (those are GT).
solution.dataset_fetch_reference: datalad install https://github.com/OpenNeuroDatasets/ds002790.git && cd ds002790 && datalad get sub-0002/anat/ && datalad get derivatives/freesurfer/sub-0002/

structural-normative-modeling.title: Normative modeling of cortical thickness
structural-normative-modeling.description: Fit a normative model of cortical thickness against age and sex on a training cohort (~200 subjects) from OpenNeuro dataset ds002790, then compute deviation (Z) scores for a held-out test set (~26 subjects).

prompt.goal: Using the FreeSurfer cortical-thickness measures and demographic covariates (age, sex), fit a normative model on a training cohort of ~200 subjects that predicts regional cortical thickness from the covariates, then compute per-region deviation (Z) scores for the held-out test subjects (~26). Report the deviation scores for the test set.
prompt.required_output: Normative deviation scores per subject (CSV/TSV)

solution.ground_truth: No single "correct" Z per se, but the GRADEABLE outcome is: the normative model fit + held-out Z-scores are well-formed and statistically sane. Reference: on healthy held-out subjects, Z-scores should be ~N(0,1) (mean ~0, sd ~1) per region since the cohort is healthy. Check: Z-matrix shape [n_test x n_ROIs], values finite, distribution approximately standard normal, no region collapsed. Model must be fit on TRAIN only (no leakage from test).
solution.pass_criterion: fs_stats cortical-thickness table and participants.tsv (age/sex) loaded; Train/test split applied (~200 train / ~26 held-out test); Normative model fit on the training cohort (thickness ~ age + sex per region); Per-region deviation (Z) scores computed for the held-out test subjects; No processing errors; logs captured
solution.output_validation: Z-scores for held-out test: shape [~26 x 37], finite, approx N(0,1) across healthy test subjects, model trained on train split only.
solution.tool_note: Context: healthy. pcntoolkit (normative modeling). Fit on train covariates->thickness, evaluate Z on held-out test.
solution.notes: [input_files] derivatives/fs_stats/data-cortical_type-aparc_measure-thickness_hemi-{lh,rh}.tsv + participants.tsv (age, sex).
solution.dataset_fetch_reference: datalad install https://github.com/OpenNeuroDatasets/ds002790.git && cd ds002790 && datalad get derivatives/fs_stats/ participants.tsv
solution.expected_files_detail: Normative deviation scores per subject (CSV or TSV, any filename; e.g. Z-scores)

solution.output_validation: Per-structure Dice on subcortical labels vs FreeSurfer aseg.
solution.tool_note: Context: healthy.
solution.dataset_fetch_reference: datalad install https://github.com/OpenNeuroDatasets/ds002790.git && cd ds002790 && datalad get sub-0007/anat/

structural-t1w-preprocessing.title: Structural T1w preprocessing (bias-correct, brain-extract, segment)
structural-t1w-preprocessing.description: Structural T1w preprocessing (bias-field correction, brain extraction, tissue segmentation) for sub-0003 of OpenNeuro dataset ds002790.

prompt.goal: Preprocess the T1-weighted image of sub-0003: apply bias-field correction, brain extraction, and tissue segmentation, producing a brain-extracted image, a brain mask, and tissue class maps.
prompt.required_output: Bias-field-corrected T1w NIfTI; Brain-extracted T1w and mask; MNI-registered T1w and transformation files

solution.ground_truth: derivatives/fmriprep/sub-0003/anat/sub-0003_desc-brain_mask.nii.gz (native) + sub-0003_dseg.nii.gz + label-{CSF,GM,WM}_probseg.nii.gz. Dice on mask + tissue overlap.
solution.pass_criterion: Bias correction, brain extraction, and MNI registration completed; All output NIfTIs present with correct geometry; No processing errors; logs captured

functional-bold-to-mni.title: BOLD-to-MNI alignment via composed transform
functional-bold-to-mni.description: Align the resting-state BOLD of sub-0019 of OpenNeuro dataset ds002790 to MNI152 space by composing the BOLD-to-anatomical and anatomical-to-MNI transforms into a SINGLE transform, and applying it in one resampling step.

prompt.goal: Align the resting-state BOLD of sub-0019 to MNI152 space. Estimate the BOLD-to-anatomical and anatomical-to-MNI transforms, then COMPOSE them into a single transform that maps BOLD directly to MNI. Output both that composed transform and the BOLD resampled into MNI space by applying the composed transform in a single interpolation.
prompt.required_output: A single composed BOLD->MNI transform (one transform file/spec, not two separate ones); The BOLD resampled into MNI152 space by applying the composed transform in one interpolation

solution.ground_truth: fMRIPrep ships the component transforms as reference: sub-0019_from-orig_to-T1w_mode-image_xfm.txt (BOLD->anat) and sub-0019_from-T1w_to-MNI152NLin2009cAsym_mode-image_xfm.h5 (anat->MNI). The MNI target is sub-0019_task-restingstate_*_space-MNI152NLin2009cAsym_boldref.nii.gz. GRADE: (1) agent outputs a SINGLE composed transform (not two files) - this is what proves the two transforms were merged into one step; (2) applying the composed transform to the BOLD produces an MNI image matching the reference MNI boldref (spatial correlation); (3) the composed transform, when applied, agrees with sequentially applying the two reference transforms (displacement-field comparison).
solution.pass_criterion: BOLD-to-anatomical transform estimated; Anatomical-to-MNI152 transform estimated; The two transforms COMPOSED into a single BOLD->MNI transform (output as one transform); BOLD resampled into MNI152 by applying the composed transform in a single interpolation; MNI-space BOLD has correct MNI dimensions; composed transform maps BOLD->MNI correctly; No processing errors; logs captured
solution.output_validation: Composed transform exists as ONE artifact; applying it lands BOLD in MNI matching the reference MNI boldref (spatial correlation); displacement agrees with the reference two-transform chain.
solution.tool_note: Context: healthy. Compose transforms (e.g. ANTs antsApplyTransforms with a transform LIST -> single output, or convertwarp/c3d_affine_tool to merge). The COMPOSED SINGLE transform is the graded output.
solution.notes: [resolves] Earlier SCORING_LIMITATION resolved: requiring the COMPOSED TRANSFORM as an explicit output makes the single-step composition gradable (a single BOLD->MNI transform can only exist if the two were merged). Distinct from structural-mni-registration (T1w->MNI image); functional-bold-to-mni grades the composed BOLD->MNI transform.
solution.dataset_fetch_reference: datalad install https://github.com/OpenNeuroDatasets/ds002790.git && cd ds002790 && datalad get derivatives/fmriprep/sub-0019/

diffusion-brain-mask.description: Brain mask from the b0 of the DWI of sub-CON02 (ses-preop) of OpenNeuro dataset ds001226

prompt.goal: Extract a b0 volume from the DWI of sub-CON02 (session ses-preop) and create a brain mask from it.
prompt.required_output: A skull-stripped brain image (NIfTI) ); A binary brain mask (NIfTI) )

solution.ground_truth: Brain mask. Reference mask.mif on OSF y2dq4. NOTE: reference mask was made via dwi2mask on the PREPROCESSED (unbiased) DWI, so the task input should be the preprocessed DWI (sub-02_den_preproc_unbiased.mif), not raw. Grade by Dice to reference mask.
solution.pass_criterion: b0 extracted from the DWI; Binary brain mask produced; geometry matches the DWI; Mask covers brain, excludes non-brain; No processing errors; logs captured
solution.tool_note: Context: healthy. Extract b0 (fslroi / dwiextract -bzero) then mask (fsl bet / mrtrix3 dwi2mask). fsl + mrtrix3 are sufficient; no need to over-list
solution.notes: [input_note] Task input = PREPROCESSED DWI (sub-02_den_preproc_unbiased.mif), because the reference mask was computed on the unbiased DWI. Do not grade a mask made from raw DWI against this reference. [subject_note] sub-CON02 (MRtrix reference series). diffusion-preprocessing/diffusion-structural-connectome use CON01 (QSIPrep clean PE).
solution.dataset_fetch_reference: datalad install ///openneuro/ds001226 && cd ds001226 && datalad get sub-CON02

solution.notes: [subject_note] sub-CON02 (MRtrix reference series). diffusion-preprocessing/diffusion-structural-connectome use CON01 (QSIPrep clean PE).

solution.pass_criterion: DWI corrected for eddy-current and motion artefacts; Corrected 4D DWI and rotated bvecs produced; No processing errors; logs captured

diffusion-dti-metrics.title: DTI core metrics (FA/MD/AD/RD)
diffusion-dti-metrics.description: Diffusion-tensor fit producing FA/MD/AD/RD scalar maps for sub-CON02 (ses-preop) of OpenNeuro dataset ds001226 (healthy control, multi-shell DWI)

prompt.goal: Fit a diffusion tensor and compute the four core DTI scalar maps - FA (fractional anisotropy), MD (mean diffusivity), AD (axial diffusivity), and RD (radial diffusivity) - for sub-CON02 (session ses-preop). Save each metric as a NIfTI map.
prompt.required_output: One or more quantitative scalar maps (NIfTI)

solution.ground_truth: Four DTI scalar maps (FA/MD/AD/RD). References under dti/ on OSF y2dq4, generated via dwi2tensor -> tensor2metric on sub-02_den_preproc_unbiased.mif. Grade each map by spatial correlation to reference (masked). Input = preprocessed DWI.
solution.pass_criterion: Diffusion tensor fit on the appropriate (lower) b-shells with bvals/bvecs and a brain mask; FA, MD, AD, RD maps produced as NIfTI; geometry matches the input DWI; FA values within [0,1]; diffusivities non-negative and in plausible range; Numerical safety applied (clip negative eigenvalues; NaN/Inf -> 0; mask out non-brain); No processing errors; logs captured
solution.output_validation: Content checks: FA in [0,1], MD/AD/RD finite non-negative, plausible white-matter FA ~0.4-0.8 in core WM. Geometry matches input. Any filename
solution.tool_note: Context: healthy. FSL dtifit, MRtrix3 dwi2tensor+tensor2metric, or DIPY tensor model. DTI fit uses the lower b-shells (b<=1200); the b=2800 shell is for higher-order models, not the tensor
solution.notes: [subject_note] sub-CON02 (MRtrix reference series). diffusion-preprocessing/diffusion-structural-connectome use CON01 (QSIPrep clean PE).
solution.dataset_fetch_reference: datalad install ///openneuro/ds001226 && cd ds001226 && datalad get sub-CON02
solution.expected_files_detail: One or more quantitative scalar maps (NIfTI, any filename; e.g. FA, MD, CBF, ALFF, ReHo)

diffusion-fod-peaks.title: Multi-fibre orientation peaks (CSD)
diffusion-fod-peaks.description: CSD multi-fibre modelling with peak extraction (4D peaks NIfTI) from the multi-shell DWI of sub-CON02 (ses-preop) of OpenNeuro dataset ds001226

prompt.goal: From the multi-shell DWI of sub-CON02 (session ses-preop), estimate the fibre response function, compute fibre orientation distributions (constrained spherical deconvolution), and extract the peak directions as a 4D NIfTI peaks image.
prompt.required_output: Fibre orientation model output

solution.ground_truth: CSD fibre-orientation peaks. Reference peaks.mif on OSF y2dq4 (sh2peaks on wmfod_norm.mif). Input = provided normalized FODs (wmfod_norm.mif) OR preprocessed DWI. Grade by peak-direction agreement in WM.
solution.pass_criterion: Response function estimated; FODs computed via CSD (uses the high b-shell); Peak directions extracted as a 4D NIfTI (e.g. peaks.nii.gz); Peaks geometry matches the DWI; vectors unit-normalised; Peak directions plausible in canonical tracts (e.g. left-right in corpus callosum, inferior-superior in CST); No processing errors; logs captured
solution.output_validation: Score by PEAK DIRECTION in known tracts, not raw SH coefficients: in the corpus callosum the primary peak should be ~left-right; in the corticospinal tract ~inferior-superior. Coarse but real. Peaks 4D NIfTI is far more scorable than SH-coefficient FODs
solution.tool_note: Context: healthy. CSD via MRtrix3 (dwi2fod), FSL bedpostx, or DIPY. Multi-fibre/CSD benefits from the high b-shell (b=2800)
solution.notes: [input_note] Provide the normalized WM FOD (wmfod_norm.mif) as input, OR the preprocessed DWI if testing the full CSD->peaks chain. Reference peaks derived from wmfod_norm. [subject_note] sub-CON02 (MRtrix reference series). diffusion-preprocessing/diffusion-structural-connectome use CON01 (QSIPrep clean PE). [note] Reframed from raw-FOD output (unscorable SH coefficients) to peak-direction output per expert review
solution.dataset_fetch_reference: datalad install ///openneuro/ds001226 && cd ds001226 && datalad get sub-CON02
solution.expected_files_detail: Fibre orientation model output (any filename/format; e.g. per-voxel fibre orientations, FODs, or bedpostX directory)

diffusion-preprocessing.title: DWI preprocessing (QSIPrep)
diffusion-preprocessing.description: QSIPrep DWI preprocessing (denoise + distortion/eddy via AP+PA) to MNI152NLin2009cAsym for sub-CON01 (ses-preop) of OpenNeuro dataset ds001226

prompt.goal: Preprocess the diffusion-weighted data of sub-CON01 (session ses-preop) using QSIPrep. Note: the reverse phase-encoded (PA) acquisition is a b0 fieldmap for susceptibility distortion correction; QSIPrep expects such short reverse-PE scans in the fmap/ directory as an EPI fieldmap (dir-PA_epi) with an IntendedFor entry pointing at the AP DWI, rather than in dwi/. Organize the data accordingly and run QSIPrep to produce the preprocessed DWI.
prompt.required_output: A derivatives directory with BIDS-derivatives layout (any name); Preprocessed NIfTI(s) and JSON sidecars; QC report or visual summary (HTML or PNG — tool-dependent)

solution.ground_truth: QSIPrep preprocessed DWI with SDC applied (verified: pepolar_qc.tsv + topupsummary present). GT files on OSF q7v8c: qsiprep/groundtruth/sub-CON01_desc-preproc_dwi.nii.gz (+.bval/.bvec + desc-brain_mask.nii.gz). Grade by similarity to reference + confirm SDC applied. Not voxel-exact (version differences).
solution.pass_criterion: QSIPrep completed on the BIDS DWI; Denoising + distortion + eddy/motion correction applied; Preprocessed DWI + updated bvals/bvecs produced; QC report generated; No processing errors; logs captured
solution.output_validation: TWO components: (1) reorganization is BIDS-valid; (2) preprocessed DWI exists with SDC applied (check report), compared to reference on OSF q7v8c. Not voxel-exact.
solution.tool_note: Context: healthy. QSIPrep BIDS-App: --participant-label, output-resolution, uses AP+PA for SDC
solution.notes: [dataset_setup] RESOLVED design: the prompt STATES the domain fact (PA is a b0 fieldmap, QSIPrep wants it in fmap/ as dir-PA_epi + IntendedFor) but the agent must EXECUTE the reorganization + run. This tests real BIDS-manipulation + pipeline competence without testing obscure dataset-trivia (the needed knowledge is given). Two gradable components: (1) BIDS-valid reorganization - fmap/ dir-PA_epi (.nii.gz +.json only, NO bval/bvec), correct IntendedFor path, PA removed from dwi/; (2) QSIPrep ran and produced a preprocessed DWI with SDC applied. Grader can distinguish failure modes (reorg failed vs QSIPrep failed vs full success). Reference GT produced the same way - see DWI_GT_GENERATION_PLAN.md. [subject_note] sub-CON01 (clean reverse-PE metadata; CON02 PA PE is mislabeled i-). [tool_specific_operational] QSIPrep is a BIDS-App: --participant-label, --output-resolution, uses AP+PA for SDC. May require a FreeSurfer license ($FS_LICENSE) for its anatomical workflow - same license dependency as aslprep. Without a license it degrades to --dwi-only and "documented space" weakens. This is a tool-specific requirement -> QSIPrep SKILL.md, and the environment must provide FS_LICENSE
solution.dataset_fetch_reference: datalad install ///openneuro/ds001226 && cd ds001226 && datalad get sub-CON01

diffusion-structural-connectome.title: DWI structural connectome (QSIRecon)
diffusion-structural-connectome.description: QSIRecon structural connectome (orientation -> tractography -> ROI-to-ROI matrix) for sub-CON01 (ses-preop) of OpenNeuro dataset ds001226

prompt.goal: Reconstruct a structural connectome from preprocessed DWI using QSIRecon for sub-CON01 (session ses-preop): estimate fibre orientations, run tractography, and produce an ROI-to-ROI connectivity matrix using a standard atlas.
prompt.required_output: A derivatives directory with BIDS-derivatives layout (any name); Preprocessed NIfTI(s) and JSON sidecars; QC report or visual summary (HTML or PNG — tool-dependent)

solution.ground_truth: QSIRecon structural connectome: sub-CON01_AAL116_connectivity.mat (116x116, AAL116 nodes). Pipeline: MRtrix3 no-ACT, MSMT-CSD + iFOD2 tractography + SIFT2 weighting. Reference on OSF q7v8c under qsirecon/groundtruth/. Node labels in atlas-AAL116_dseg.tsv. WEAKEST-graded: verify matrix is 116x116, symmetric, non-negative, plausible density/sparsity - structure/presence not exact edge weights (tractography is stochastic + SIFT2 weights vary run-to-run).
solution.pass_criterion: QSIRecon reconstruction completed; Whole-brain tractography produced; ROI-to-ROI connectivity matrix produced (N_ROIs x N_ROIs) with a named atlas; Matrix symmetric/undirected; non-negative weights; No processing errors; logs captured
solution.output_validation: HONEST LIMITATION: the connectome matrix is the LEAST precisely scorable output in the DWI family. Two reasonable pipelines produce different matrices with no ground truth for which is correct. Gradable only at STRUCTURE level: square N_ROIs x N_ROIs, symmetric, non-negative, finite, non-degenerate (not all-zero/constant), atlas labels present. This is structure-plus-presence grading, weaker than the content grading used elsewhere. Kept because connectome generation is a major DWI use case (coverage), but do not over-claim its scoring precision
solution.tool_note: Context: healthy. QSIRecon recon workflow (e.g. mrtrix_multishell_msmt) -> connectome. Typically consumes QSIPrep output
solution.notes: [input_note] QSIRecon input = the QSIPrep derivatives directory. For the reference, fetch the QSIPrep output tarball from OSF q7v8c (qsiprep/qsiprep-CON01.tar.gz), untar, point qsirecon at it. [dataset_setup] Consumes diffusion-preprocessing QSIPrep output (sub-CON01). Same dataset-setup lineage as diffusion-preprocessing. [subject_note] sub-CON01. [atlas] AAL116 (116 nodes -> 116x116 connectome). Atlas choice defines expected matrix dimensions. [recon_spec] mrtrix_multishell_msmt_noACT + --atlases AAL116 (output dir: qsirecon-MRtrix3_act-None)
solution.dataset_fetch_reference: datalad install ///openneuro/ds001226 && cd ds001226 && datalad get sub-CON01

diffusion-tractography-tdi.title: White-matter tractography + track-density image
diffusion-tractography-tdi.description: Whole-brain tractography + track-density image (TDI scalar NIfTI) on the DWI of sub-CON02 (ses-preop) of OpenNeuro dataset ds001226

prompt.goal: Perform whole-brain white-matter tractography on the DWI of sub-CON02 (session ses-preop), then convert the tractogram to a track-density image (TDI): a voxel-wise scalar NIfTI giving streamline density on the DWI grid.
prompt.required_output: Per-tract density or segmentation maps (NIfTI) ); Tract streamlines file

solution.ground_truth: Whole-brain tractography track-density image. Reference tdi.mif on OSF y2dq4 (tckgen ACT 1M streamlines -> tckmap). Inputs provided on OSF: 5tt_coreg.mif, gmwmSeed_coreg.mif, wmfod_norm.mif. Grade by spatial correlation of TDI to reference (tractography is stochastic; grade density map not individual streamlines).
solution.pass_criterion: Fibre orientations estimated; whole-brain tractogram generated; Tractogram converted to a track-density image (tckmap -template) as a 3D NIfTI; TDI geometry matches the DWI grid; intensities non-negative; High track density in major white-matter pathways (e.g. corpus callosum, CST), low in CSF/GM; No processing errors; logs captured
solution.output_validation: Score the TDI scalar map (not the raw tractogram): non-negative, geometry matches DWI, high density in major WM tracts and low in CSF/cortex. A tckmap TDI turns an unscorable tractogram into a gradable voxel-wise map
solution.tool_note: Context: healthy. MRtrix3 (tckgen after dwi2fod CSD), FSL probtrackx (after bedpostx), or DIPY. Whole-brain tractogram
solution.notes: [input_note] Provide preprocessed inputs (wmfod_norm.mif, 5tt_coreg.mif, gmwmSeed_coreg.mif) from OSF y2dq4 - the task is tractography+TDI, not full preprocessing. TDI graded by spatial correlation (stochastic streamlines). [subject_note] sub-CON02 (MRtrix reference series). diffusion-preprocessing/diffusion-structural-connectome use CON01 (QSIPrep clean PE).
solution.dataset_fetch_reference: datalad install ///openneuro/ds001226 && cd ds001226 && datalad get sub-CON02
solution.expected_files_detail: Per-tract density or segmentation maps (NIfTI, any filename); Tract streamlines file (any filename/format — tool-dependent; e.g..tck,.trk)

quantitative-susceptibility-mapping.title: QSM reconstruction
quantitative-susceptibility-mapping.description: Run QSMxT on magnitude + phase data to produce a susceptibility map; verify spatial coherence in deep grey matter

prompt.goal: Reconstruct a quantitative susceptibility map (QSM) for sub-1 (session ses-20170705) from the QSMxT tutorial dataset (OSF ). The data is supplied as gradient-echo (T2*-weighted) DICOMs with magnitude and phase, plus a T1w; organise it as needed and produce the susceptibility map.
prompt.required_output: A quantitative susceptibility map (NIfTI) in ppm.

solution.ground_truth: QSM susceptibility map (Chimap.nii), QSMxT on ru43c sub-1.
solution.pass_criterion: DICOMs converted to BIDS with part-mag + part-phase T2starw NIfTIs present; QSM reconstructed from phase + magnitude via phase unwrapping, background field removal, and dipole inversion; Susceptibility map produced in NIfTI; geometry consistent with input GRE; Values within plausible susceptibility range (approx. -0.5 to +0.5 ppm in tissue); No processing errors; logs captured
solution.tool_note: Context: healthy. QSMxT is the single QSM tool (SEPIA not available). Effectively named
solution.notes: [tool_specific_operational] QSMxT workflow: (1) convert DICOM->BIDS with `dicom-convert` (QSMxT ships it); the conversion must identify the T2*w/GRE series for QSM vs the T1w series for segmentation - not in the DICOM header, so QSMxT guesses from ProtocolName (patterns *qsm*/*t2starw*) or the user specifies. (2) Run `qsmxt <bids_dir>` with a premade pipeline (e.g. --premade fast/gre) and **--auto_yes** to avoid the INTERACTIVE CLI that otherwise hangs a non-interactive run. (3) Optional --do_segmentation/--do_analysis for ROI stats [known_obstacle] The interactive CLI is the key trap: `qsmxt bids_dir` without --auto_yes launches an interactive setup and will hang an agent run. Knowing --auto_yes (and the dicom-convert step) is exactly the operational knowledge the QSMxT skill should supply - and whether it does is the signal we measure
solution.dataset_fetch_reference: osf -p ru43c clone. && tar xf osfstorage/dicoms-unsorted.tar && rm -rf osfstorage
solution.expected_files_detail: A quantitative susceptibility map (NIfTI, any filename; e.g. *Chimap*.nii*, *QSM*.nii*, or *susceptibility*.nii*)

quantitative-swi.title: Phase unwrapping + SWI with ClearSWI
quantitative-swi.description: ClearSWI susceptibility-weighted imaging (SWI + minIP) from the gradient-echo magnitude and phase of sub-1 (ses-20170705) from OSF dataset ru43c

prompt.goal: Using ClearSWI, produce a susceptibility-weighted image (SWI) and a minimum-intensity projection (minIP) for sub-1 (session ses-20170705) from the OSF dataset. The data is supplied as gradient-echo magnitude and phase (with echo time metadata); organise it as needed and produce the SWI and minIP.
prompt.required_output: A susceptibility-weighted image (NIfTI) and a minimum-intensity projection (NIfTI).

solution.ground_truth: SWI image + minimum-intensity projection (swi.nii, mip.nii), ClearSWI on ru43c sub-1.
solution.pass_criterion: Phase unwrapped; SWI produced by combining magnitude with a phase mask; Minimum-intensity projection (minIP) produced; SWI and minIP geometry consistent with the input GRE; No processing errors; logs captured
solution.tool_note: Context: healthy. ClearSWI; SEPIA is a possible alternative NOTE: this dataset is single-echo T2*w; ClearSWI is multi-echo-oriented but runs on single-echo. SEPIA is an alternative
solution.notes: [tool_specific_operational] ClearSWI runs two ways: (a) the clearswi command-line binary (bin/clearswi, NO Julia needed), or (b) a short Julia script (CLEARSWI.jl): readmag/readphase -> Data(mag,phase,header,TEs) -> calculateSWI -> createMIP -> savenii. Needs magnitude + phase NIfTI and the echo time(s) TEs. DICOM->BIDS conversion same as QSMxT (dicom-convert). GE data needs readphase(...; fix_ge=true) - not needed for this tutorial data [known_obstacle] Echo time(s) must be supplied to ClearSWI; multi-echo needs the TE list. The command-line clearswi binary is the agent-friendly path (avoids Julia). Unlike LCModel, SWI is genuinely scriptable/CLI-runnable
solution.dataset_fetch_reference: osf -p ru43c clone. && tar xf osfstorage/dicoms-unsorted.tar && rm -rf osfstorage

clinical-ms-lesion-segmentation.title: MS white-matter lesion segmentation
clinical-ms-lesion-segmentation.description: Automatic MS white-matter lesion segmentation for patient01 of the open_ms_data cross-sectional dataset (FLAIR-based), with Dice against the provided consensus ground truth

prompt.goal: Segment the white-matter (FLAIR-hyperintense) lesions for patient01 of the open_ms_data cross-sectional dataset. Use the co-registered data (all sequences registered to FLAIR space, with a provided brain mask). Produce a binary lesion mask in FLAIR space, restricted to the brain mask. The dataset provides an expert consensus ground truth that may be used to report Dice, but the segmentation must be produced automatically.
